key_themes: FSD autonomy progress and real-world performance
Q1 2026 earnings anticipation with focus on guidance and capex
Divided Wall Street views vs. contrarian bullish sentiment on X
summary: TSLA sentiment has improved to mildly positive as the stock near $401 snapped an 8-week losing streak amid renewed AI/autonomy excitement ahead of Q1 2026 earnings on April 22. Strong FSD real-world results, Terafab AI chip efforts, and Optimus updates are driving bullish narratives and contrarian buying interest on X despite earlier negative sentiment, high valuation concerns, and cautious analyst targets around $415 average. Investors are focused on margins, FSD monetization, Robotaxi timelines, and whether AI capex will pressure near-term free cash flow.
sources: 15+ recent news articles (Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, Motley Fool, Reuters), 30+ X posts (semantic + keyword searches since early April 2026)
